data.content:
Plan for documenting the interactive agent session protocol

Scope
- This beat should publish the canonical markdown contract for interactive agent integrations based on the shipped shared runtime in foolery-51f8. No runtime behavior changes are planned unless a small discoverability or conformance gap appears while documenting the current contract.

Relevant current implementation
- Capability declarations and transport/result knobs: src/lib/agent-session-capabilities.ts
- Dialect-specific prompt building and stream normalization: src/lib/agent-adapter.ts
- Shared runtime lifecycle, stdin handling, watchdog, and process-group termination: src/lib/agent-session-runtime.ts
- Initial-child follow-up and close wiring: src/lib/terminal-manager-initial-io.ts
- Post-exit validation, retry classification, rollback, and queue/terminal enforcement: src/lib/terminal-manager-take-loop.ts, src/lib/terminal-manager-take-iteration.ts, src/lib/terminal-manager-workflow.ts
- Existing conformance coverage: src/lib/__tests__/agent-session-capabilities.test.ts, src/lib/__tests__/agent-adapter.test.ts, src/lib/__tests__/agent-session-runtime-lifecycle.test.ts, src/lib/__tests__/terminal-manager-error-retry-outcome.test.ts, src/lib/__tests__/terminal-manager-abort.test.ts

Implementation steps
1. Read the shipped shared-runtime code and extract the actual session contract, explicitly separating agent-specific transport details from the normalized lifecycle that wrapper code depends on.
2. Add a new design artifact at docs/interactive-agent-session-protocol.md as the source of truth for later integrations.
3. Structure the document around the required contract points: declared agent capabilities; startup handshake; prompt delivery mode; normalized stream-event contract; turn-complete vs step-complete/session-end; in-session follow-up prompts; stdin close/drain rules; wrapper exit/draining behavior; post-exit backend validation; watchdog, retry, and abort semantics.
4. Make the lifecycle state machine explicit and normative: start -> prompt_sent -> active_turn -> turn_complete -> optional_follow_up -> draining -> exited -> validated.
5. State clearly that stream silence is never enough to declare success. Success requires both an agent/session completion signal and a post-exit backend state check/classification.
6. Link the new document from docs/MANIFEST.md if needed so later implementation work has a stable entry point.
7. If documenting the current behavior exposes a wording gap or uncovered edge case, add the narrowest matching test/doc touchups instead of broad runtime changes.

Expected file changes
- New: docs/interactive-agent-session-protocol.md
- Likely small doc integration: docs/MANIFEST.md
- Possible narrow conformance updates only if needed: src/lib/__tests__/agent-session-capabilities.test.ts, src/lib/__tests__/agent-adapter.test.ts, src/lib/__tests__/agent-session-runtime-lifecycle.test.ts, src/lib/__tests__/terminal-manager-error-retry-outcome.test.ts, src/lib/__tests__/terminal-manager-abort.test.ts

Complexity and risks
- Complexity: medium. The work is mostly synthesis, but the contract is currently split across capability declarations, stream normalization, runtime lifecycle, and post-exit workflow validation.
- Primary risk: conflating turn completion with workflow success. The document must preserve that a result event ends a turn, while workflow success is decided only after process exit plus backend state validation/classification.
- Secondary risk: interactive Claude follow-up semantics differ from one-shot Codex, Gemini, Copilot, and OpenCode behavior. The capability table must make those differences explicit.
- Dependency note: foolery-51f8 is already shipped, so this beat should document the current shared runtime rather than redesign it.

Test strategy
- Cross-check every required protocol statement against the shipped runtime and the focused adapter/runtime/terminal-manager tests above.
- If documentation clarifies behavior that is not currently asserted, add focused test coverage only in the most relevant existing test file.
- Run targeted tests for touched contract areas, then full lint/typecheck/test/build only if the implementation for this beat ends up touching code beyond docs.

Hierarchy decision
- No child knots created for this plan. The scoped deliverable is a single bounded documentation artifact plus a small discoverability link, so further decomposition would add coordination cost without reducing delivery risk.
